countries.name: United States
facts: The United States has the world's largest economy by nominal GDP
It is home to more than 30,000 public libraries, more than McDonald's locations
The US invented the internet, the airplane, and the telephone
Americans eat about 100 acres of pizza every day
The US has won more Olympic medals than any other country
countries.climate: Varied — from arctic in Alaska to tropical in Hawaii and Florida; temperate across most of the continental states

countries.name: United Kingdom
facts: The UK invented the World Wide Web — Tim Berners-Lee was British
The Queen's Guard outside Buckingham Palace has been in place since 1660
The UK is made up of four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland
The first public railway in the world opened in England in 1825
Big Ben is actually the name of the bell inside the Elizabeth Tower, not the tower itself
countries.climate: Temperate oceanic — mild, wet, and changeable weather throughout the year

countries.name: India
countries.language: Hindi, English, and 21 other official languages
facts: India is the world's largest democracy
Home to the ancient Indus Valley Civilization
The game of Chess was invented in India
India has the most vegetarians in the world
The number zero was invented in India
countries.climate: Tropical monsoon, varies from alpine in the north to tropical in the south

countries.name: Australia
facts: Australia is the only country that is also a continent
Australia has more kangaroos than people
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system
Australia is home to 21 of the world's 25 most venomous snakes
Canberra was purpose-built as the capital as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne
countries.climate: Mostly arid or semi-arid; temperate in the southeast and southwest; tropical in the north

countries.name: Canada
facts: Canada has the longest coastline of any country in the world
Canada produces about 71% of the world's maple syrup
Ice hockey was invented in Canada
Canada has more lakes than all other countries combined
The word 'Canada' comes from the Huron-Iroquois word 'kanata', meaning village
countries.climate: Varies from arctic in the north to humid continental in the south; temperate on the west coast

countries.name: China
facts: China has the world's oldest continuous civilization, dating back over 5,000 years
The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles
China invented paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass
China is the world's largest manufacturer and exporter of goods
Giant pandas are found only in China in the wild
countries.climate: Extremely diverse — from subarctic in the northeast to tropical in the south; desert in the west

countries.name: Japan
facts: Japan has the world's oldest company — Kongo Gumi, a construction firm founded in 578 AD
Mount Fuji is Japan's highest peak and an active volcano
Japan has more vending machines per person than any other country
The Japanese bullet train (Shinkansen) travels at up to 200 mph
Japan experiences about 1,500 earthquakes every year
countries.climate: Mostly temperate with four distinct seasons; subtropical in the south; subarctic in Hokkaido

countries.name: France
facts: France is the most visited country in the world, welcoming about 90 million tourists a year
The Eiffel Tower was originally intended to be torn down after 20 years
France has over 1,200 varieties of cheese
The French invented the metric system
France is the largest country in the European Union by area
countries.climate: Temperate in the north and northwest; Mediterranean in the south; continental in the east

countries.name: Germany
facts: Germany is home to over 1,500 different types of beer and 1,300 breweries
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg in Germany
Germany has the largest economy in Europe
Kindergarten, a concept from Germany, literally means 'children's garden'
Germany has more zoos than any other country in the world
countries.climate: Temperate and marine; cool and wet in the north; continental with cold winters in the east; warmer in the south

countries.name: Brazil
facts: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth largest in the world
The Amazon rainforest, the world's largest tropical forest, covers about 60% of Brazil
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times
Brazil is the world's largest producer of coffee
The Amazon River carries more water than any other river on Earth
countries.climate: Mostly tropical; equatorial in the Amazon basin; semi-arid in the northeast; temperate in the south

countries.name: Mexico
facts: Mexico City is built on the site of the ancient Aztec capital Tenochtitlan
Mexico is the birthplace of chocolate — the Aztecs were the first to make chocolate drinks
Mexico has the world's largest pyramid by volume — the Great Pyramid of Cholula
The Mexican flag's eagle, snake, and cactus symbol comes from an ancient Aztec legend
Mexico is one of the world's 17 'megadiverse' countries for biodiversity
countries.climate: Varies from tropical in the south to desert in the north; temperate in the highlands

countries.name: South Africa
countries.language: Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, English, and 7 other official languages (11 total)
facts: South Africa is the only country in the world with three capitals
South Africa has 11 official languages — the most of any country in the world
The world's first heart transplant was performed in Cape Town in 1967
South Africa is home to the largest land mammal (elephant) and the tallest (giraffe)
South Africa produces about 80% of the world's platinum
animals: Big Five (Lion, Elephant, Rhino, Leopard, Buffalo)
countries.climate: Mostly semi-arid; subtropical along the east coast; Mediterranean in the southwest

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.language: English (official), Hausa, Yoruba, Igbo, and over 500 others
facts: Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa
Nigeria has the largest economy in Africa
Nollywood, Nigeria's film industry, produces more movies than Hollywood
Nigeria is one of the world's top oil producers
Lagos is the largest city in Africa by population
countries.climate: Tropical — equatorial in the south, tropical in the center, arid in the far north

countries.name: Egypt
facts: Egypt is home to one of the world's oldest civilizations, dating back over 5,000 years
The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only surviving wonder of the ancient world
Ancient Egyptians invented writing using hieroglyphics
The Nile is the longest river in the world and flows through Egypt
Ancient Egyptians were the first to use calendar with 365 days
countries.climate: Desert — hot and dry; the Nile Valley is more moderate; Mediterranean coast is milder

countries.name: Russia
facts: Russia is the largest country in the world, covering 11 time zones
Lake Baikal in Russia is the world's deepest lake and holds 20% of Earth's fresh surface water
Russia was the first country to send a human to space — Yuri Gagarin in 1961
The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway in the world at over 9,000 km
Russia has the world's largest forest — the Siberian Taiga
countries.climate: Continental — mostly subarctic and arctic; mild and humid in western Russia; extreme cold in Siberia

countries.name: Italy
facts: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country
The Roman Empire was founded in Italy and once ruled most of Europe
Italy is home to two independent countries within it — Vatican City and San Marino
Pizza and pasta, enjoyed worldwide, originated in Italy
The Italian Renaissance produced masters like Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo
countries.climate: Mediterranean in the south and coast; continental in the Po Valley; alpine in the north

countries.name: Spain
facts: Spain is the second largest country in the European Union by area
Spain has the second highest number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Europe
La Tomatina is a famous Spanish festival where people throw tomatoes at each other
Spanish is the world's second most spoken native language
Spain is famous for flamenco dancing, which originated in Andalusia
countries.climate: Mediterranean in the coast and south; semi-arid in the southeast; oceanic in the north; continental interior

countries.name: South Korea
facts: South Korea has the fastest average internet speed in the world
South Korea is the birthplace of the K-pop and K-drama global phenomenon
South Korea went from one of the world's poorest countries to a high-income economy in just 60 years
Koreans celebrate two birthdays — the day they are born and the lunar new year
Samsung, Hyundai, and LG are all South Korean companies
countries.climate: Temperate — hot and humid summers; cold and dry winters with heavy snowfall in some regions

countries.name: Argentina
facts: Argentina is the birthplace of the tango dance
Argentina has the largest proven reserves of shale gas outside the United States
Argentinian soccer legend Lionel Messi is widely considered the greatest player of all time
Patagonia, a vast wilderness, covers about a third of Argentina
Argentina is the eighth largest country in the world by area
countries.climate: Subtropical in the northeast; arid in the west; temperate in the Pampas; subpolar in Patagonia

countries.name: New Zealand
countries.language: English, Māori, New Zealand Sign Language
facts: New Zealand was the first country to give women the right to vote, in 1893
New Zealand has more sheep than people — about 6 sheep for every person
New Zealand is home to the kiwi bird, which cannot fly
The Lord of the Rings films were entirely shot in New Zealand
New Zealand is one of the last places on Earth to be settled by humans
countries.climate: Temperate maritime — mild and wet; more subtropical in the north; cooler and snowier in the south

countries.name: Singapore
facts: Singapore is the only city-state in Southeast Asia
Singapore is consistently ranked as one of the world's safest and least corrupt countries
Singapore Changi Airport has been voted the world's best airport multiple times
Singapore has no natural freshwater resources and recycles nearly all its water
Despite its tiny size, Singapore has the world's third highest GDP per capita
countries.climate: Tropical rainforest — hot, humid, and rainy year-round with no distinct seasons

countries.name: United Arab Emirates
facts: The Burj Khalifa in Dubai is the tallest building in the world at 828 meters
The UAE went from a desert with small fishing villages to a modern nation in about 50 years
Dubai has the world's largest artificial islands — the Palm Jumeirah
The UAE is one of the world's top ten oil exporters
About 89% of people living in the UAE are expats from other countries
countries.climate: Hot desert — extremely hot summers; mild winters; very low rainfall throughout the year

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
facts: Saudi Arabia is home to Mecca and Medina, the two holiest cities in Islam
Saudi Arabia has the world's second largest oil reserves
The Empty Quarter (Rub' al Khali) in Saudi Arabia is the world's largest continuous sand desert
Saudi Arabia was the first Gulf country to give women the right to drive, in 2018
Saudi Arabia has no rivers or permanent lakes — fresh water comes from desalination plants
countries.climate: Hot desert — extreme heat in summer; mild winters; very little rainfall

countries.name: Kenya
facts: Kenya is home to the Great Rift Valley, one of the most dramatic geographical features on Earth
The Maasai Mara hosts the Great Migration — the world's largest animal movement
Kenya is known as the birthplace of long-distance running, producing many world champions
Nairobi is the only city in the world with a national park within its borders
Kenya is home to the 'Big Five' game animals
countries.climate: Varies — tropical on the coast; arid in the interior; temperate in the highlands; semi-arid in the north

countries.name: Ghana
countries.language: English (official), Akan, Ewe, Dagbani, and about 80 others
facts: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan country to gain independence from colonial rule, in 1957
Ghana is the world's second largest producer of cocoa
The name Ghana means 'Warrior King' in the Soninke language
Ghana has one of the most stable democracies in Africa
Lake Volta in Ghana is the world's largest man-made lake by surface area
countries.climate: Tropical — hot and humid in the southwest; hot and dry in the north; two rainy seasons in the south

countries.name: Pakistan
countries.language: Urdu (national), English (official), Punjabi, Sindhi, Pashto, Balochi
facts: Pakistan is home to K2, the world's second highest mountain
The Indus Valley Civilization, one of the world's oldest, was in present-day Pakistan
Pakistan has the largest canal irrigation system in the world
The Lahore Fort and Shalimar Gardens are UNESCO World Heritage Sites
Pakistan is the world's sixth most populous country
countries.climate: Varies from arctic in the north to hot desert in the south; monsoon season in the east

countries.name: Bangladesh
facts: Bangladesh is the world's most densely populated large country
The Sundarbans, the world's largest mangrove forest, is in Bangladesh
Bangladesh is the world's leading garment exporter after China
The Royal Bengal Tiger lives in the Sundarbans
Bangladesh has over 700 rivers, earning it the name 'Land of Rivers'
countries.climate: Tropical monsoon — hot and humid; heavy rains from June to October; mild winters

countries.name: Sri Lanka
facts: Sri Lanka is known as the 'Pearl of the Indian Ocean'
Sri Lanka produces some of the world's finest tea — Ceylon Tea is famous globally
Sri Lanka has the highest literacy rate in South Asia
The ancient city of Sigiriya has a 5th century rock fortress at its centre
Sri Lanka is home to the blue whale, the largest animal on Earth, in its coastal waters
countries.climate: Tropical — hot and humid; two monsoon seasons; cooler in the central highlands

countries.name: Nepal
facts: Nepal is home to 8 of the world's 10 highest mountains, including Everest
Nepal's flag is the only national flag in the world that is not rectangular
The Buddha, Siddhartha Gautama, was born in Lumbini, Nepal
Nepal has never been colonized by a foreign power
Nepal has over 240 peaks above 6,000 meters
countries.climate: Varies dramatically — tropical in the south; alpine and arctic in the Himalayas; temperate in the middle hills

countries.name: Indonesia
facts: Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago, made up of over 17,000 islands
Indonesia is the world's fourth most populous country
Indonesia has the world's third largest tropical rainforest
Komodo dragons, the world's largest lizards, are found only in Indonesia
Indonesia sits on the 'Ring of Fire' and has over 130 active volcanoes
countries.climate: Tropical — hot and humid year-round; heavy rainfall especially in the western islands; drier in the east
